audit_items.proof_trace: `lutil_eaddr()` returns NULL on multiple error paths (sysctl failure, malloc failure, no matching Ethernet interface); the result is assigned to `nl` at line 364 but there is no NULL check before `nl[0]`..`nl[5]` are dereferenced in the snprintf call at lines 375-377 (the fallback `#else` branch). No guard exists anywhere in the call chain to prevent the dereference.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP client sends an Add request; `servers/slapd/add.c:651` calls `lutil_uuidstr()` on a platform using the fallback branch; if the OS sysctl/socket call fails (e.g., resource exhaustion or no Ethernet interface) `lutil_eaddr()` returns NULL and the subsequent `nl[0]` dereference crashes the slapd process (DoS).
